channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Interest in AI-Assisted Music Declines Most Among Young Listeners, Luminate Study Finds
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to Trending on Billboard, where we dive into the latest music trends and insights. Today, we're looking at a new study from Luminate that reveals some surprising findings about consumer interest in AI-assisted music. Let's break it down."
[Soft music transitions in]
Host: "According to the study, U.S. consumer interest in listening to AI-assisted music has declined across all age groups from May to November 2025. The biggest drop came from Gen Alpha and Gen Z, with a net decline from -6% to -16%. But what's even more interesting is that overall, U.S. listeners have had a net negative interest in listening to music made with generative AI since the first survey in May 2025, with that score becoming even more negative by November."
[Music transitions to a slightly different tone]
Host: "Now, let's look at the age categories. Millennials were the most open to listening to AI-assisted music, while Boomers were the least likely to feel positive about it. Here are the numbers:
- General population: -13% in May to -20% in November - Gen Alpha and Gen Z: -6% to -16% - Millennials: -1% to -7% - Gen X: -16% to -24% - Boomers: -34% to -38%
[Music transitions back to the main theme]
Host: "The study also found that while U.S. consumers are more likely to report negative sentiments toward AI use in music-making, about a third of respondents are ambivalent. Let's look at the breakdown:
- For song instrumentals, 10% are very comfortable, 19% somewhat comfortable, 30% indifferent, 14% somewhat uncomfortable, and 28% very uncomfortable - For song lyrics, the numbers are similar, with 10% very comfortable, 16% somewhat comfortable, 29% indifferent, 16% somewhat uncomfortable, and 29% very uncomfortable
[Music transitions to a soft outro]
Host: "These findings come at a time when top music companies and AI music firms are forging licensing deals. The study from Luminate provides valuable insights into the complex and evolving relationship between AI and music. That's all for today's episode of Trending on Billboard. Thanks for tuning in!"
[Outro music plays]
